_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 8-4 OB High Risk Delivery Transport (Revised 12/27/2022, MDHHS Reviewed 2022). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
level_display: System (All EMS Levels — MCA Optional Facility Designation)
protocol_reference: Michigan System Protocol Section 8-4: OB High-Risk Delivery Transport — MDHHS Approved 12/27/2022
purpose: MCA OPTIONAL. Govern transport destination for high-risk OB patients and patients in active labor. MCAs may designate preferred OB-capable receiving facilities. Delivery may occur en route — apply OB delivery protocols as needed. This protocol works in conjunction with clinical OB protocols.

sections.title: High-Risk OB Transport Destination
points: MCA OPTIONAL — MCA may designate preferred OB-capable receiving facilities for high-risk deliveries
High-risk OB conditions that may warrant transport to a higher-level OB facility:
— Known fetal anomaly requiring specialized NICU
Transport to closest OB-capable facility is appropriate when delivery is imminent or patient is hemodynamically unstable
Contact Medical Control when uncertainty exists about destination for high-risk OB patients

sections.title: Imminent Delivery Considerations
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-4: OB Transport — Imminent Delivery
points: If delivery is imminent (crowning visible, uncontrollable urge to push, perineal bulging): do NOT transport — prepare for field delivery
After delivery: transport mother and newborn together to OB-capable facility
Neonatal resuscitation: manage per Neonatal Protocol; contact NICU center if needed

sections.title: Air Medical Considerations
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-4: OB Transport — Air Medical
points: Consider air medical transport for: remote location with prolonged transport to OB-capable facility, hemodynamically unstable high-risk OB patient, preterm labor < 32 weeks where ground transport > 30 minutes to appropriate facility
Refer to Section 8-9: Helicopter Utilization Protocol for air medical activation criteria
Air medical is NOT appropriate for imminent delivery — manage field delivery first

mca_selections_required.description: MCA may designate specific OB-capable receiving facilities for high-risk deliveries in their jurisdiction
options: MCA-designated OB facilities listed in MCA protocols
Transport to nearest OB-capable facility; contact Medical Control for high-risk cases

deterioration_flags: Imminent delivery during transport — stop vehicle if safe; prepare for field delivery; do NOT continue transport with crowning
Eclampsia or hemodynamic compromise — contact Medical Control; consider air medical; transport to highest-level OB facility available
scope_notes: MCA OPTIONAL — MCA may designate preferred OB-capable receiving facilities
High-risk OB: preterm < 34 weeks, eclampsia, abruption, prolapsed cord, multiple gestation, fetal anomaly
Imminent delivery: manage field delivery — do NOT transport with crowning
Air medical: consider for remote location, unstable patient, preterm < 32 weeks with prolonged transport
Transport mother and newborn together after delivery
